mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and lower corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable consumer prices indicates robust household purchasing power without inflationary pressure, generating positive equity returns as consumer spending drives corporate revenues.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0% annually, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements and short-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery periods, or the 5-day return correlation drops below 0.05 in walk-forward validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which transmit to broader equity markets through supply chain demand and wealth effects over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of rising HOUST months.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 240.0, inflation expectations force monetary tightening and compress real corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment indicates a healthy labor market expansion that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k monthly, or the interaction term loses predictive significance with p-value above 0.15 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth deceleration below 2.0% signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDP deceleration fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 2.0%.
